citations.description: Ticket f9ce-7098-9f17-433e has status 'open', no comments, and no runtime attestation recorded.
The batch position_map is built and threaded through, but no timing data or comparison against baseline is present.

findings.criterion: Whole-store rebar verify-identity runtime <= pre-f482 baseline on the real store.
findings.detail: This criterion requires an empirical runtime measurement on the real store to confirm the regression is resolved. It is untagged (codebase-verifiable per the rules), and no runtime measurement, benchmark result, CI timing, or attestation exists anywhere in the codebase or ticket (the ticket has no comments and status is 'open'). While the batch-map mechanism is correctly implemented in code, the criterion's actual claim — that the runtime meets or beats the pre-f482 baseline — cannot be verified from repository contents alone.
findings.remediation: Record proof as a ticket comment naming: (a) the CI run URL/id where the whole-store verify-identity job completed, (b) the measured runtime, (c) the pre-f482 baseline for comparison, and (d) the date/run. Tag the criterion [operator-attested] if runtime measurement is not feasible from code.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
